creator: ish@amail.plala.or.jp
text:
(In reply to Cy Schubert from comment #8)

Thank you for reply.
I'm busy with the care of my parents, so I'm sorry for the late reply.

1. wpa_cli reconfigure
Failed to connect to non-global ctrl_ifname: (nil)  error: No error: 0

My /etc/wpa_supplicant.conf is as follows.
# cat /etc/wpa_supplicant.conf
ap_scan=1

network={
  ssid="XXXXXXXX"
  scan_ssid=1
  bssid=XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX
  proto=RSN
  key_mgmt=WPA-EAP
  pairwise=CCMP
  group=CCMP
  eap=TLS
  identity="XXXXXX"
  ca_cert="/etc/cacert.pem"
  client_cert="/etc/newcert.pem"
  private_key="/etc/newkey.pem"
  private_key_passwd="XXXXXXXX"
}

2-1. Without lagg
  wlan is connected at startup.

2-2. With lagg but wlan is not member
  I can't configure lagg0 without wlan0.

ifconfig_lagg0="laggproto failover laggport ue0 192.168.X.X netmask 255.255.255.0"

lagg0: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        ether XX:XX:XX:XX
        inet 192.168.X.X netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 192.168.X.255 
        laggproto failover lagghash l2,l3,l4
        laggport: wlan0 flags=0<>   <=== Why is wlan0 a member ?
        laggport: ue0 flags=5<MASTER,ACTIVE>
        groups: lagg 
        media: Ethernet autoselect
        status: active
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>

3. netif_wait_timeout=60 and netif_wait_if="ue0" are meaningless.
Yes. It's my mistake.
I was used patched /etc/rc.d/netif with those variables about 1 years ago.
But recent 12-current can be working with standard /etc/rc.d/netif and these variables are meaningless. Sorry.
